phase: Phase 2.7 mapper v0.1 implementation spec — ZS + boundary nodes
status: documentation only; no mapper code changes
purpose: Concrete implementation spec for ZS, moralBoundaries.ethnic_racial, and moralBoundaries.gender composites — exact columns, wording, codes, directionality, weights per cycle.
explicitly_not_in_this_spec: No engine / selector / topK / browser / dist / output / live-data / candidate / era-context edits.
No edits to generated smoke outputs.
forbidden_universal_inputs: pid7 for any core PRISM position node
Partisan-camp-loaded items (Kavanaugh, ACB, Trump impeachment) for any non-political_camp target

zs_composite.implementation_rule: Each item maps to a per-respondent signal in [-1, +1]; -1 → ZS-low, +1 → ZS-high. Composite = weighted average of per-item signals; converted to peaked posPosterior over 1-5 ZS scale via mapper distribution helper.

items.battery_prompt: If the federal government had to balance the budget, in what order would you do these things?
items.direction: Ranked first → very light ZS-low; Ranked third → light ZS-high

2016.verdict: Single weak item only — 2016 ZS effectively remains at fallback prior with tiny directional nudge

items.exact_wording: OVER THE PAST YEAR, has your household's annual income...?
items.direction: Decrease (4-5) → ZS high; Increase (1-2) → ZS low; 3 → neutral

items.battery_prompt: For each of the following tell us whether you support or oppose these decisions
items.exact_wording: Withdraw the United States from the Trans-Pacific Partnership trade agreement, a free trade agreement that included the U.S., Japan, China, Australia, New Zealand, Canada, Chile, others.
items.direction: Support → ZS high (trade-as-zero-sum); Oppose → ZS low

items.exact_wording: Reduce LEGAL immigration by 50 percent over the next 10 years
items.direction: Support → ZS high (anti-immigration as labor-market protectionism)
cross_loads: CU primary + mB.national primary; ZS is secondary cross-load

2020.verdict: Pass v0.1 threshold; mapper flag uncertainty `medium`

items.exact_wording: OVER THE PAST YEAR, have the prices of everyday goods and services...?
items.direction: Increase (1-2) → ZS high (cost-of-living up); Decrease (4-5) → ZS low
items.implementation_critical_note: CC20_303 (income) and CC24_303 (prices) have OPPOSITE direction signs because the question subjects are different. Mapper resolver MUST NOT share a single transform between the two columns.

items.exact_wording: Extend the tax cuts enacted in 2017, which reduced individual and corporate income tax rates and limited deductions on mortgage interest and state and local taxes
items.direction: Mid (anti-tax-cut Republicans + anti-tax-cut progressives both Oppose for opposite reasons)
items.reason_skipped: Direction-ambiguous on ZS (opposite reasons converge to same answer)

items.exact_wording: Raise the corporate income tax rate from 21 percent to 28 percent
items.reason_low_weight: Primary load is MAT; ZS is incidental and double-counts MAT routing if weighted heavily

items.exact_wording: Allow tax rates on those earning $400,000 or more a year to rise to 35 percent
Spend $150 billion a year for 8 years on construction and repair of roads and bridges, rail, public transit, airports, water systems, broadband internet, and electric grid
items.direction: Support → light ZS-low (infrastructure as positive-sum public good)

2024.verdict: Lower than 2020; mapper flag uncertainty `medium`. Single primary item drives most signal.

ethnic_racial_composite.scale: 0-3 salience scalar (one-sided; cannot push salience below baseline)
ethnic_racial_composite.deferred_reason: Other cycles lack post-George-Floyd context for police-reform items; need ANES VCF0830 for primary signal

items.exact_wording: Do the police make you feel...?
items.direction: Race-conditional. For non-white respondents: 3-4 → ethnic_racial salience high. For white respondents: largely uninformative.
items.signal_formula: if respondent.race in {black, asian, amerindian, other_single, two_or_more} OR respondent.hispanic: signal = max(0, (response_value - 2) / 2); else: signal = 0
items.implementation_critical_note: RACE-CONDITIONAL gating required. White-respondent answers do not contribute. Mapper resolver must read respondent.race field and gate the item appropriately.
items.circularity_risk: none (race demographic is not pid7 or vote-related)

items.battery_prompt: Do you support or oppose each of the following proposals?
items.exact_wording: Eliminate mandatory minimum sentences for non-violent drug offenders.
items.direction: Support → ethnic_racial salience high (criminal-justice-reform framing has explicit racial-disparity component); Oppose → 0
cross_loads: Light CD (criminal-justice reform); composite handles each target separately

items.exact_wording: Decrease the number of police on the street by 10 percent, and increase funding for other public services
items.direction: Support → ethnic_racial salience high (post-George-Floyd defund framing); Oppose → 0

items.exact_wording: Ban the use of choke holds by police
items.direction: Support → ethnic_racial salience high; broadly popular ~70%+
items.reason_reduced: Broadly popular across all races; weak discriminator

items.exact_wording: Create a national registry of police who have been investigated for or disciplined for misconduct.

items_excluded.reason: Body cameras — universally popular ~95%; zero discriminator
Increase police 10% — direction inverts to anti-reform
End DOD military equipment — anti-militarism / PRO cross-load too strong
Allow suits against police — civil-liberty / PRO cross-load too strong

2020.v02_validation_required: Re-validate 2020 directionality against ANES once available; post-George-Floyd context made these items unusually salient

gender_composite.scale: 0-3 salience scalar (one-sided; cannot push salience below baseline)
gender_composite.fallback_reason: No CCES per-year items audited support v0.1 gender-salience composite for older cycles

items.battery_prompt: Over the past two years, Congress voted on many issues. Do you support each of the following proposals? [Favor / Oppose]
items.exact_wording: Amend federal laws to prohibit discrimination on the basis of gender identity and sexual orientation.
items.direction: Favor → gender salience high; Oppose → near-fallback (one-sided)
cross_loads: CD primary (already routed); gender is parallel routing not cross-load

items.exact_wording: Require equal pay for women and men who are doing similar jobs and have similar qualifications.
items.direction: Favor → gender salience high (one-sided, half-strength because broad consensus ~80% Favor)
items.notes: Gender-load is STRONGER than CD-load per prior audit; CD composite uses at 0.5×, gender composite uses at 0.5× independently — not double-counting because composites are per-target

items.exact_wording: Ban Transgender People in the Military
items.direction: DIRECTION-AGNOSTIC: Support OR Oppose → gender salience high (any non-neutral answer); missing/skipped → 0
items.implementation_critical_note: Both anti-trans and pro-trans respondents show high gender salience — only apolitical respondents push salience low. Use direction-agnostic transform: |response_value - neutral|, NOT signed direction.

items.battery_prompt: Do you support or oppose each of the following bills before Congress?
items.exact_wording: Require that all federal agencies recognize same-sex marriages and interracial marriages.
items.direction: Support → gender salience high (one-sided, mirrors CC20_350a; reduced strength because more about marriage equality than gender identity per se)
cross_loads: CD primary (already routed); ethnic_racial light (interracial marriage component)

items.exact_wording: Provide permanent resident status to children of immigrants who were brought to the United States by their parents (also known as Dreamers). Provide these immigrants a pathway to citizenship if they meet the citizenship requirements and have committed no crimes
items.direction: Light universalist signal, not gender-specific; marginal gender contribution only
items.notes: Included only because 2024 has so few gender items; primary loads are CU + MOR, gender is minor

2024.verdict: Single primary-item composite; mapper flag uncertainty `medium`

resolver_signal_fn_contract.race_conditional_unsafe_to_high: if respondent.race ∈ non-white set OR respondent.hispanic: max(0, (response_value - 2) / 2); else 0

terminology: moralBoundaries.ethnic_racial and moralBoundaries.gender are canonical compound moral-circle boundaries per ADR-006. Engagement is referenced only as a downstream concern — separate 1D continuous scalar per ADR canon — not surfaced by any item specified here. Legacy code identifiers (CC*_*, pid7, ideo5) appear only as CCES variable names.

revision_history.description: Initial implementation spec for ZS, mB.ethnic_racial (2020 only), mB.gender (2020 + 2024) v0.1 composites.
